channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In the early going, Trump 2.0 approach on foreign policy is to talk loudly and carry a big stick
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump in his first week back in the White House has offered an early preview to his second-term foreign policy approach: Talk loudly and wield a big stick.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Hegseth at the Helm
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court leaves in place Mississippi’s voting ban for people convicted of some crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday left in place Mississippi’s Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance political advisers join Ramaswamy ahead of expected Ohio governor run
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy is set to launch his bid mid-February, per the source familiar, to succeed term-limited Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine (R) in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Three of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks set for hearings
item.description: More hearings have been scheduled for Trump’s Cabinet nominees, marking a significant week ahead for some of his more controversial picks.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up more confirmations as House GOP talks Trump agenda
item.description: (The Hill) — The Senate is set to churn through more of President Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees this week, with a handful of his controversial picks set to field questions during their confirmation hearings. Tulsi Gabbard, Trump’s pick to be director of national intelligence; Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Target gets booted from Twin Cities Pride festival after rolling back DEI efforts
item.description: Twin Cities Pride executive director Andi Otto says the nonprofit made the decision because Target is not standing up “against those who are fighting against us.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Marshall 'absolutely' comfortable with Trump’s purge of inspectors general
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kan.) suggested that, like Reagan, Trump could potentially rehire some of the watchdog officials after cleaning house at the agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Ramaswamy's expected run for Ohio governor would test experienced Republicans and tradition
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Fresh off of his unexpected departure from President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, Vivek Ramaswamy has set his sights on becoming governor of his home state.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: This is how Trump organized his Oval Office decor
item.description: Every president gets to decorate the Oval Office to their liking — but sometimes, they keep the decor of their predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Trump's embattled pick for defense secretary, wins Senate confirmation
item.description: Vice President JD Vance cast a tie-breaking vote as Hegseth overcame allegations of sexual assault, public drunkenness and questions of financial mismanagement to win Senate approval.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Trump has tossed a lot of Biden policies. But there’s one AI measure he wants to keep
item.description: President Trump says he likes Biden’s idea to open up federal lands for AI data centers. His White House is looking for ways to ensure U.S. dominance in the sector.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk tried to turn the salute controversy into a joke
item.description: The incident only lasted seconds, but it sparked what has become a global debate about how to interpret what Musk did. Then Musk started posting Nazi-themed jokes.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump pardons anti-abortion activists who blockaded clinic entrances
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Thursday he would pardon anti-abortion activists convicted of blockading abortion clinic entrances.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada, 17 other states sue Trump administration over birthright citizenship order
item.description: Attorneys general from 18 states, including Nevada, sued the Trump administration Tuesday to stop it from seeking to eliminate birthright citizenship, a protection guaranteed in the U.S. Constitution.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis: North Florida could see several inches of snow
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis warned of potential snowfall this week, amid the freezing temperatures impacting northern Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gen. Milley portrait taken down from Pentagon
item.description: The portrait, which had just been unveiled Jan. 10, hung in the Joint Chiefs hallway next to those of other former chairs.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Immigration top priority for Trump and Florida lawmakers
item.description: Solving the illegal immigration crisis is a priority for both President Donald Trump and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
